meta.description: EDEN Service Validator — unified service profiles and global keyword lists. This file is the single source of truth, replacing services_default_queries.csv, validation_config.json, and the hardcoded 'patterns' dict in Validator.py.

global._comment: Cross-cutting keyword lists used by _classify_html_response(). Formerly in validation_config.json.

OAI-PMH.title: Open Archives Initiative - Protocol for Metadata Harvesting

Static OAI.title: OAI Static Repository
Static OAI.comment: A static XML file, not a live OAI-PMH server. The base URL should point directly to the static repository XML file.

REST.title: Representational State Transfer
REST.comment: Generic REST endpoint. A 400 Bad Request on the base URL is acceptable — it indicates the server is live and responding to REST-style requests, even if the root path requires parameters.

ERDDAP.title: ERDDAP
ERDDAP.comment: ERDDAP's /index.json returns a JSON object with a 'table' key listing all datasets.

SOAP.title: Simple Object Access Protocol
SOAP.comment: Appending '?wsdl' to a SOAP endpoint URL typically returns the WSDL service description.

SWORD.title: Simple Web-service Offering Repository Deposit
SWORD.comment: SWORD is primarily for depositing content and will often be behind an auth wall. A 401 Unauthorized is expected and indicates the endpoint is live.

OpenAPI.title: OpenAPI Specification
OpenAPI.comment: Tries /openapi.json on the endpoint base URL. Multiple spec versions (2.0, 3.0.x, 3.1.x) are all captured by the same body signatures.

IIIF Search API.title: IIIF Content Search API
IIIF Search API.comment: The API should return a JSON error document (e.g. a IIIF error manifest) when queried without parameters. Body signatures are tentative and may need refinement with real examples.

IIIF Presentation API.title: IIIF Presentation API
IIIF Presentation API.comment: Body signatures are tentative and may need refinement with real examples.

LDN.title: Linked Data Notifications
LDN.comment: Inbox URL discovered via HEAD/GET on target resource: Link header rel="http://www.w3.org/ns/ldp#inbox". GET on Inbox returns JSON-LD listing notifications via ldp:contains. POST to Inbox sends notification (Content-Type: application/ld+json); expects 201 Created or 202 Accepted. A 405 Method Not Allowed on the inbox itself is accepted as valid — it confirms the inbox exists but GET requires authentication.

OpenSearch.title: Open Search
OpenSearch.comment: No canonical endpoint or query syntax. The endpoint URL must point directly to the OpenSearch Description Document (OSD). Look for <link rel="search" type="application/opensearchdescription+xml"> in HTML to discover the OSD URL. Root element is <OpenSearchDescription> in namespace http://a9.com/-/spec/opensearch/1.1/.

AWS-OpenSearch.title: AWS OpenSearch (Elasticsearch-compatible REST API)
AWS-OpenSearch.comment: AWS OpenSearch (and Elasticsearch) exposes a JSON REST API. The /_cluster/health endpoint returns cluster status JSON with cluster_name and status fields. Note: unrelated to the OpenSearch (OSD) description document format.

FAIRiCat.title: FAIRiCat: Supporting Discovery of a Repository's Interoperability Affordances
FAIRiCat.comment: Static JSON Link Set document (application/linkset+json) discovered via a Link header with rel="api-catalog" and profile="https://signposting.org/FAIRiCat/". The endpoint URL must point directly to the FAIRiCat document. Top-level key is "linkset" containing an array of objects each with an "anchor" and link-relation-named members (service-doc, service-desc, service-meta).

LAS.title: Live Access Server
LAS.comment: No unified capabilities endpoint. ProductServer.do is the official HTML health check (returns a page starting with 'This is the ... LAS Product Server'). GUI metadata available at /las/getCategories.do (returns JSON). Format for data products is controlled via LAS request properties, not HTTP Accept.

AMPQ.title: Advanced Message Queuing Protocol
AMPQ.comment: STUB: AMQP is a binary wire protocol; HTTP-based probing is not applicable. Validation strategy TBD.

MQTT.title: Message Queuing Telemetry Transport
MQTT.comment: STUB: MQTT is a TCP-based binary protocol; HTTP-based probing is not applicable. Validation strategy TBD.

XMPP.title: Extensible Messaging and Presence Protocol
XMPP.comment: STUB: XMPP is an XML-stream TCP protocol; HTTP-based probing is not applicable. Validation strategy TBD.

TAPIR.title: TDWG Access Protocol for Information Retrieval
TAPIR.comment: Explicit ping operation. Response is XML with root <response xmlns="http://rs.tdwg.org/tapir/1.0"> containing <pong>. The TAPIR namespace must be the default namespace in all messages. Default operation (no op=) returns metadata.

DIGIR.title: Distributed Generic Information Retrieval
DIGIR.comment: Requires HTTP POST with a minimal XML DiGIR request body. Response is XML with root <response> in the DiGIR namespace. The probe body field contains the minimal metadata request.

OGC-CSW.title: OGC Catalogue Service for the Web
OGC-CSW.comment: CSW 2.0.2 uses 'version' (not 'acceptVersions') for GetCapabilities. Root element is csw:Capabilities in namespace http://www.opengis.net/cat/csw/2.0.2. CSW 3.0 uses namespace .../csw/3.0.

OGC-WMS.title: OGC Web Map Service
OGC-WMS.comment: WMS 1.3.0 root element is WMS_Capabilities in namespace http://www.opengis.net/wms. WMS 1.1.1 uses WMT_MS_Capabilities. Accept header not part of WMS contract — format is controlled by the request, not HTTP negotiation.

OGC-WMTS.title: OpenGIS Web Map Tile Service
OGC-WMTS.comment: OWS Common requires ACCEPTVERSIONS (not VERSION) for GetCapabilities. Root element is <Capabilities> in default namespace http://www.opengis.net/wmts/1.0. Also supports RESTful access via .../1.0.0/WMTSCapabilities.xml.

OGC-WFS.title: OGC Web Feature Service
OGC-WFS.comment: OWS Common requires ACCEPTVERSIONS for GetCapabilities. Root element is wfs:WFS_Capabilities in namespace http://www.opengis.net/wfs/2.0. WFS 1.x uses namespace http://www.opengis.net/wfs (no /2.0).

OGC-SOS.title: OGC Sensor Observation Service
OGC-SOS.comment: SOS 2.0 uses acceptVersions for GetCapabilities. Root element is sos:Capabilities in namespace http://www.opengis.net/sos/2.0. Endpoint paths vary by binding (/kvp, /pox, /soap). SOS 1.0 uses version= instead of acceptVersions=.

OGC-WCS.title: OGC Web Coverage Service
OGC-WCS.comment: OWS Common requires acceptversions for GetCapabilities (not version=). Root element is wcs:Capabilities in namespace http://www.opengis.net/wcs/2.0. WCS 1.0 uses WCS_Capabilities in namespace http://www.opengis.net/wcs.

IVOA-SCS.title: Simple Cone Search
IVOA-SCS.comment: SCS returns a VOTable with QUERY_STATUS INFO element. SR=0 means zero search radius (metadata probe).

IVOA-SIA.title: Simple Image Access
IVOA-SIA.comment: SIA returns a VOTable with RESOURCE type=results. MAXREC=0 returns metadata only (no rows).

IVOA-SSA.title: Simple Spectral Access
IVOA-SSA.comment: SSA FORMAT=METADATA returns a VOTable describing supported parameters. REQUEST=queryData is mandatory per spec.

IVOA-SLAP.title: Simple Line Access Protocol
IVOA-SLAP.comment: SLAP FORMAT=METADATA returns a VOTable with service metadata. getCapabilities is reserved but not fully defined in SLAP 1.0.

IVOA-TAP.title: Table Access Protocol
IVOA-TAP.comment: TAP /capabilities is mandatory per spec (VOSI). Response contains <capability standardID='ivo://ivoa.net/std/TAP'> with TAPRegExt type.

IVOA-VOSpace.title: VOSpace, the IVOA interface to distributed storage
IVOA-VOSpace.comment: VOSpace uses VOSI /availability endpoint. Response is XML with <availability> root in VOSIAvailability namespace containing <available>true|false</available>.

NetCDF.title: NetCDF (OPeNDAP)
NetCDF.comment: UNSUPPORTED: NetCDF is a file format, not a network service protocol. It cannot be validated via HTTP requests. Marked unsupported so the validator returns 'Unsupported/Cannot be verified' gracefully. text/html is too generic to trust alone — original profile kept for reference.

FTP.title: File Transfer Protocol
FTP.comment: Real ftp:// URLs are validated via ftplib, not HTTP requests (use_ftp_validator: true). HTTP URLs that return an FTP-style directory listing (Apache mod_autoindex, etc.) are flagged as invalid (detect_http_wrapped_ftp: true). Status codes for FTP are synthesised HTTP-like values: 200 = successful connection + directory listing, 550 = path not found or connection error.
